system.description:
A wax-sealed letter addressed to 'The Watch-Commander, Stairwatch.' The seal bears the King's cipher — but not Harald's personal seal. Someone else with court access sent this.

The letter warns Stairwatch to: 'Detain any travellers matching the descriptions provided and hold them for questioning on behalf of the Crown.' The description of the party is disturbingly accurate.

Use: Present this letter to Stairwatch officials — either as evidence of corruption (warning Lew that Hrafnborg's security is compromised), or to bluff your way past checkpoints by claiming to be the escort who recovered it from a delay. A hard Presence test to make the bluff convincing.

GM Notes

Found: In the Frozen Messenger's satchel on the mountain road (Beat 4b, The Frozen Messenger interlude). The courier has been dead roughly four days.

Context: This letter was not sent by Harald — it was generated by Dreyfus's influence operating through Wyrd-channels and a compromised court official. The initials 'V.A.' link to Voss Ashwright, a shadow figure in the Chainwarden corruption network. Leave this ambiguous: the PCs cannot prove Dreyfus's involvement yet, and the letter could equally appear to be a misguided court functionary acting on partial orders.

The satchel also contains: 2 days' rations (frozen, edible once thawed) and a Chainwarden map of Titan's Stair with hazard annotations (+1 advantage (second chance) on one navigation test in the next montage).

system.corruptionDescription: Using this artifact costs 1 temporary corruption.
